frameworks.name: active vision / oculomotor sampling
frameworks.one_line: fixational eye movements treated as an active sampling strategy that shapes retinal input.

frameworks.name: spatial-frequency analysis of retinal input
frameworks.one_line: characterizes how drift reshapes the temporal-spectral content of retinal stimulation.

frameworks.name: static optics account of acuity
frameworks.one_line: view that resolution is set by eye optics and photoreceptor anatomy alone.

synopsis.core_question: Do fixational eye movements contribute to high-acuity perception beyond what static ocular optics allow?
key_assumptions: retinal image motion during fixation can be precisely measured and stabilized
high-acuity discrimination depends on the spatial-frequency content available on the retina
ocular drift can be treated as a controlled sampling process
manipulations: retinal image stabilization vs. natural fixational motion during a high-acuity task
across-observer variation in fixational eye-movement precision
key_findings: fixational eye movements tailor retinal input to the dominant spatial frequencies of the stimulus
high-acuity performance scales with the precision of an observer's fixational eye movements
computational analyses show ocular drift improves discriminability of fine spatial detail beyond static optics
interpretations: oculomotor drift acts as an active sampling strategy that enhances fine pattern vision
high visual acuity is co-determined by motor control, not by optics and anatomy alone
